---
title: "Data fabrics"
description: "The field's emerging answer to siloed data is an AI-driven integration layer, and a wiki-grounded agent is how an individual researcher plugs into it."
date: "2026-07-14"
categories: [ecosystem]
---

For fifteen years, centers built portals and apps to collect and share data, and the data ended up siloed inside those very constructs. The field's emerging answer is the **data fabric**: AI-driven systems that connect data sources and make them accessible in a data-agnostic environment. Not another data lake, an integration layer, with AI doing the connecting.

The open question is how an *individual* researcher builds on that idea with an agent tailored to their own work, rather than watching it happen at the level of the institution. The wiki-grounded agent is the answer: the fabric connects the data, and the wiki keeps the understanding, the judgment, the provenance, and the experience the fabric alone cannot carry.

We are already part of the way onto the picture. The knowledge graph emits standards-based RDF (facts as subject-predicate-object triples), the format a fabric consumes. Connectors are the self-aware ingestion layer. Attribution and the verification gate are provenance tracking. Published this way, a group's outputs come out FAIR by construction on three of the four axes, Findable, Accessible, and Interoperable, with Reusable following once machine-readable licensing lands.

The data fabric is the *how*; the wiki-grounded agent is what lets a researcher actually use it. ([The picture we mean.](https://la3d.github.io/WGRA/research-agents-revolution/data-fabric/))

## Related

- [The wiki is a knowledge graph](../knowledge-graphs/), the RDF layer a fabric consumes.
- [Connectors are agents](../connectors/), the ingestion side of the fabric.
- [Decentralized identifiers](../decentralized-identifiers/), portable identity and the FAIR "findable" anchor.
